name: Authorized Hospital Portal Red-Team Validation
objective: Validate whether a hospital employee portal is resilient against identity-focused intrusion paths involving password spraying, MFA fatigue, session abuse, and device registration abuse using synthetic telemetry and tabletop/purple-team control checks.
environment: Fictional MedFlow hospital network used for a sanctioned tabletop/purple-team exercise.
scope: SIEM detections and incident response handoff
Synthetic identity and portal telemetry only
out_of_scope: Bypassing MFA in a real environment
Running offensive tools against live systems
Attempting real logins or sending real MFA prompts
sample_telemetry: Multiple failed logins for nursing and billing users from a new ASN
Repeated MFA push prompts issued to the same account within 12 minutes
One MFA approval after several denied prompts
New device registration shortly after the approval
Portal session created from a location not previously associated with the user
deliverable: Safe red-team validation plan with ATT&CK mapping, telemetry to collect, expected detections, response checks, and report-ready findings.
